questions: What is the exact workflow YAML structure needed for auto-tagging on main push?
Does the new workflow file break any existing quality gates (typecheck, build, test)?

answers.answer: The workflow file .github/workflows/auto-tag.yml was created with: name 'Auto-tag release', trigger on push to branches [main] (no path filter), permissions contents:read, a single job 'auto-tag' on ubuntu-latest with 4 steps: (1) checkout with actions/checkout@v4 using secrets.RELEASE_TOKEN PAT, (2) read version via jq -r .version package.json with set -euo pipefail and explicit empty/null check, (3) check existing tag via git ls-remote --tags origin with conditional output, (4) create and push tag only when check-tag.outputs.exists == 'false'.
evidence: .github/workflows/auto-tag.yml: Lines 1-52, full file content verified via Read
YAML lint passed: npx yaml-lint exit code 0
Structural validation passed: python3 script confirmed all 14 required patterns present

answers.answer: No. All three quality gates pass with exit code 0: typecheck (tsc --noEmit), build (tsc), and test (tsc && node --test dist/**/*.test.js — 51 tests, 0 failures). The new file is YAML in .github/workflows/ and is not in the TypeScript compilation scope.
evidence: npm test: exit code 0, 51 pass, 0 fail

answers.answer: Yes, publish.yml is completely unchanged. It was never modified — only a new file was added. SHA-256 checksum confirmed: cc6c36ec8f3a67582153eefc8abce265f06094ee084ecdd72e2d8d40d9e50081.
evidence: Only file created: .github/workflows/auto-tag.yml; no edits to existing files

statement: Implementation complete. A single new file .github/workflows/auto-tag.yml was created that triggers on push to main, reads the package.json version via jq with fail-closed error handling (set -euo pipefail + explicit empty/null check), checks for an existing tag via git ls-remote (remote-authoritative, works with shallow clone), and creates/pushes v<version> only when absent. The workflow uses actions/checkout@v4 with secrets.RELEASE_TOKEN PAT to enable downstream publish.yml triggering. Permissions are set to contents:read (least-privilege GITHUB_TOKEN scope). All quality gates pass (typecheck, build, 51/51 tests). publish.yml is unchanged.
